title: Doctrina Donroe
summary: The article critiques Trump's foreign policy actions, particularly the intervention in Venezuela and threats toward Greenland, coining the term 'Donroe Doctrine' as a parody of the Monroe Doctrine. The author argues Trump lacks any coherent long-term strategy, operating instead on personal impulses and insecurities, while warning that Europe must wake up to the reality that NATO effectively no longer exists.
talking_points: Trump's Venezuela intervention was primarily driven by Marco Rubio's political ambitions to build his legacy and position as Trump's successor over Vance
The so-called 'Donroe Doctrine' has no real principles - everything is improvised based on Trump's personal mood and insecurities
The US under Trump treats the entire Western Hemisphere as its property, echoing but distorting the 1823 Monroe Doctrine
Europe has invested too little in Greenland and will likely lose it, while NATO exists only in name
Trump's actions may have positive side effects like pushing Europe toward federalization, but the underlying approach is dangerous and unprincipled
author: Andrei Colțea
